mnemonics.title: Stream Class Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: Think of 'i' as input (comes INTO program) and 'o' as output (goes OUT of program). So istream = input stream, ostream = output stream. 'f' prefix = file (fstream, ifstream, ofstream). 'stringstream' = stream for strings.

mnemonics.title: File Modes Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: PIN: P-positions I-nput N-output modes - APPend, IN, OUT. TRUNC truncates (destroys). ATE goes to end (At The End). BInary = B for bytes (raw).

mnemonics.title: Stream States - FATE
mnemonics.content: FATE: Fail (recoverable), Bad (fatal), good (everything OK), EOF (end of file). Remember: Fate has Fail and Bad outcomes.

acronyms.usage: Base class name and general term for C++ stream system
Indicator when no more data can be read from stream
C++ class for file input/output operations

visualTips.visualization: Imagine water flowing through a pipe. Data flows from source to destination through the stream. The stream is the pipe, stream buffer is the water, and the source/destination are the endpoints (keyboard, file, memory).

Think of a tree: ios_base is the trunk, ios branches out, then istream and ostream are major branches, with iostream being where they meet. fstream, ifstream, ofstream are specialized leaves on the ostream/istream branches for files.
Use arrow direction: >> points INTO your program (extraction/input), << points OUT of your program (insertion/output). Remember: 'cin >> x' means arrow points TO x (into variable).
Think of file modes as door directions: in=enter (read), out=exit (write), app=push from behind (append), trunc=hammer smash (destroy existing), binary=lock open (raw mode).

analogies.analogy: Streams are like plumbing pipes - just as water flows through pipes from source to destination, data flows through streams from input devices to your program (istream) or from your program to output devices (ostream).
Stream buffers are like water tanks between the source/destination and the program - they accumulate data (fill) before releasing it (drain), improving efficiency.

analogies.concept: File Opening Modes as Door Operations
analogies.analogy: Opening a file is like opening a door - ios::in opens it for entering (reading), ios::out opens it for leaving (writing), ios::app knocks before adding to what's inside.
stringstream is like a factory that can take raw materials (different data types) and produce formatted string products, or take string input and process it into different data types.
